title: caroxylon divaricatum
intro: Caroxylon divaricatum, also known as the prickly saltwort, is a type of succulent shrub native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Caroxylon divaricatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or poultices for topical application, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
